name: Vietnam
tagline: Ancient temples, bustling streets, and pho

scenarios.description: Immigration and customs at Vietnamese airports

arrivalSteps.title: Visa Preparation / E-Visa
arrivalSteps.description: Vietnam's e-Visa (90-day single or multiple entry) is available to citizens of all countries via evisa.xuatnhapcanh.gov.vn. Apply at least 3 business days before travel. Many nationalities receive visa-free entry for 30–90 days (policy expanded in 2023). Print or save your e-Visa approval letter.

arrivalSteps.title: Health Declaration (if applicable)
arrivalSteps.description: Complete the health and quarantine declaration form if required (currently only mandatory for passengers from disease-alert regions). A combined entry declaration form may be available on board or at the terminal.

arrivalSteps.title: Passport Control
arrivalSteps.description: Join the foreigners queue. Present your passport, e-Visa (or visa exemption document), and completed arrival card. Officers will stamp your passport with the permitted duration of stay. State the exact address of your first accommodation as it will be recorded.

arrivalSteps.title: Biometrics
arrivalSteps.description: Fingerprints are collected at the immigration counter for most foreign nationals. This is standard procedure and takes under one minute.

arrivalSteps.title: Baggage Claim
arrivalSteps.description: Collect your baggage from the designated carousel. Keep the baggage claim stubs as they may be checked before you exit.

arrivalSteps.title: Customs Declaration
arrivalSteps.description: Duty-free: gifts up to VND 10 million, 200 cigarettes, 1.5L spirits over 20%, or 2L spirits 20% and below. Declare cash/gold above USD 5,000 equivalent. Prohibited: narcotics (death penalty), weapons, reactionary literature, and certain electronics requiring import permits.

fallbackAlerts.message: Ignore taxi touts at the airport exit. Use the official taxi desk inside arrivals or book via the Grab app.
Drug trafficking in Vietnam carries the death penalty. Do not carry packages for strangers.

phrases.nativeScript: Tôi không có gì cần khai báo.
phrases.romanized: Toi khong co gi can khai bao.

scenarios.description: Pagoda etiquette, dress codes, and offering customs

arrivalSteps.title: Remove Shoes at the Entrance
arrivalSteps.description: Shoes must be removed before entering the main prayer hall. Leave them neatly at the rack.

arrivalSteps.title: Dress Modestly
arrivalSteps.description: Cover shoulders and knees. Many temples will refuse entry to visitors in shorts or sleeveless tops.

arrivalSteps.title: Do Not Touch Statues
arrivalSteps.description: Do not touch, climb on, or take selfies with Buddha statues — this is deeply disrespectful.

fallbackAlerts.message: Incense is an offering, not decoration. Handle it with both hands and bow slightly when placing it.
Ask permission before photographing monks or people praying. Many find it intrusive.

scenarios.description: Grab bikes, metered taxis, and road safety

arrivalSteps.title: Use Grab App
arrivalSteps.description: Grab is the dominant ride app and displays fixed prices upfront. Far safer than hailing on the street.

arrivalSteps.title: Wear a Helmet on Motorbikes
arrivalSteps.description: Wearing a helmet on motorbike taxis is legally required and can save your life in busy traffic.

arrivalSteps.title: Cross Roads Carefully
arrivalSteps.description: Traffic lights are often ignored. When crossing on foot, walk slowly and steadily — traffic flows around you.

fallbackAlerts.message: Helmet use is legally required on all motorbike taxis. Refuse the ride if no helmet is provided.
Agree on a price or use the meter before boarding any unapp'd taxi. Ask the driver to turn it on.

phrases.english: Please take me to this address.
phrases.nativeScript: Vui lòng đưa tôi đến địa chỉ này.
phrases.romanized: Vui long dua toi den dia chi nay.

scenarios.description: Bargaining, food safety, and vendor etiquette

arrivalSteps.title: Bargaining is Normal
arrivalSteps.description: At wet markets and souvenir stalls, prices are negotiable. Smile, start low, be friendly.

fallbackAlerts.message: Counterfeit branded goods are widespread. Purchasing them is technically illegal, though rarely enforced for tourists.

phrases.nativeScript: Bạn có thể giảm giá không?
phrases.romanized: Ban co the giam gia khong?
